title: Participant Waiver & Release
description: Liability waiver releasing organizers from injury claims during events, sports, or physical activities.
whatsCovered: Assumption of risk for physical and outdoor activities
Release of organizers from claims of general negligence
Agreement not to sue the event hosts for injury or loss
Signer certifies physical fitness to participate
whenToUse: Use this liability waiver for gyms, events, or workshops to protect organizers from customer injury lawsuits.

fields.label: Activity / Sport Description
fields.placeholder: e.g. Outdoor Rock Climbing and Hiking Course

clauses.heading: PARTICIPANT WAIVER & RELEASE
clauses.body: This agreement or policy (the "Agreement") is executed as of {effective_date} (the "Effective Date"), by and between {party_a_name}, residing/located at {party_a_address} ("First Party"), and {party_b_name}, residing/located at {party_b_address} ("Second Party").

clauses.heading: 1. Core Framework
clauses.body: This agreement establishes the legal rights, bylaws, transfers, waivers, or consent structures for the Participant Waiver & Release. The parties agree to adhere to the terms and declarations set out below.

clauses.heading: 2. Physical Activity Negligence Releases
clauses.body: Participant assumes all injury risks during activity: {activity_description}. Participant releases event hosts from negligence claims.

clauses.heading: General Legal Terms
clauses.body: This document constitutes the entire understanding between the parties. Any amendments must be made in writing and executed by both parties.

clauses.heading: Governing Law
clauses.body: This Agreement shall be governed and interpreted under the laws of the State/Country of {governing_state}.

faq.question: Can I customize this legal template?
faq.answer: Yes, you can edit this template using the online editor, and add custom schedules or exhibits.

faq.question: Is this agreement binding?
faq.answer: Yes, once signed by all parties, it becomes a legally binding contract under state laws.
